facts.primaryTitle: The Eight-Foot Woman Called His Name
facts.setting: Clara first saw the woman's white hat moving above a two-metre wall.
criticalEvents: Clara first saw the woman's white hat moving above a two-metre wall.
Then the voice calling from the garden sounded exactly like her grandmother.
Clara Bennett was a teenager visiting her grandparents when the story began in a rural Japanese village surrounded by summer fields.
The first reports did not describe an obvious monster or violent attack.
facts.threat: Clara first saw the woman's white hat moving above a two-metre wall.
facts.primaryReveal: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Did Clara Bennett defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
